description: Build and deploy site to Cloudflare Pages after fixing retellCallId binding
Disable Vite sourcemap generation (build.sourcemap: false) to reduce build time

asi.hypothesis: Sourcemap generation adds non-trivial overhead to Vite production builds; disabling should reduce total build+deploy time
asi.next_action_hint: Separate build-only timing from deploy variance; next try should either (a) use direct ./node_modules/.bin/wrangler path to avoid npx startup overhead, or (b) optimize actual build time by reducing server-bundle size (heavy deps like three.js, reveal.js bloat worker bundle)
asi.rollback_reason: Total time increased to 21.7s vs baseline 13.9s. Deploy overhead dominated due to npx wrangler resolution and network variance. Build itself was similar speed (~6.3s server build). Sourcemap change did not meaningfully improve build time because Vite already defaults sourcemaps to false in production for client builds.

description: Disable Vite gzip size reporting (build.reportCompressedSize: false) and use local wrangler path to avoid npx overhead

asi.hypothesis: Gzip size computation and npx overhead add visible time to the build+deploy cycle
asi.next_action_hint: Need current baseline at HEAD because prior best (13.9s) was at older commit; try a new untouched baseline run. After establishing current best, try build optimizations like rollupOptions.manualChunks, lighter dependencies, or reducing server bundle size
asi.rollback_reason: Total time 19.6s still higher than historical best 13.9s; gzip reporting/npx were not the bottleneck. Build time is stable around 8.5s; deploy variance dominates

description: Establish baseline at current HEAD (93fd84a) for build+deploy time

asi.hypothesis: Current HEAD baseline is ~20s total (build ~8.5s + deploy ~12s); prior 13.9s best was at older commit with less code
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on server build time (~6.2s) which dominates. Investigate if heavy client-only deps (three.js, d3-geo, reveal.js) are being bundled into SSR build. Consider marking them as external in build.rollupOptions or using dynamic imports gated by browser checks.

description: Disable CSS code splitting (build.cssCodeSplit: false) to reduce output file count

asi.hypothesis: CSS code splitting creates many small CSS files, increasing file count and deploy overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Try bundling all external server deps with ssr.noExternal + wrangler --no-bundle to eliminate wrangler bundling step entirely; OR try reducing heavy external deps like @clerk/backend
asi.rollback_reason: Total time unchanged at ~20.9s. CSS files were still split (70 files uploaded). Build time not meaningfully affected by CSS code splitting.

description: Disable Rollup tree-shaking (rollupOptions.treeshake: false) to speed up build

asi.hypothesis: Disabling Rollup tree-shaking reduces build CPU time because Rollup skips side-effect analysis
asi.next_action_hint: Opposite experiment: try treeshake: 'smallest-side-effects' for smaller bundle and faster deploy; OR reduce bundle size by removing dead code/test routes; OR investigate if deploy time correlates with worker bundle size
asi.rollback_reason: Total time 23.4s worse than baseline 20.8s. Build was faster (client 1.69s, server 5.54s) but deploy slowed down significantly. Deploy time is sensitive to bundle size.

description: Remove /test/voice-agent route to reduce build module count

asi.hypothesis: Removing the /test/voice-agent route reduces module count and speeds up build+deploy
asi.next_action_hint: Build-only optimizations are hard to measure due to deploy variance (~±2s). Try bundling external deps during Vite build with ssr.noExternal to reduce wrangler bundling work; OR measure build-only time separately from deploy
asi.rollback_reason: Total time 20.6s vs baseline 20.8s — no meaningful difference. Build actually slightly slower (9.06s vs 8.6s), likely noise. Removing test route did not meaningfully reduce build or deploy time.

description: Bundle all @clerk/* and small utils into SSR output via ssr.noExternal regex

asi.hypothesis: Bundling external server deps during Vite build reduces wrangler's bundling work during deploy, improving total time
asi.next_action_hint: Try wrangler pages deploy --no-bundle to skip worker bundling entirely now that most deps are inlined; OR try bundling remaining small external deps like @sveltejs/kit internal modules if safe; OR re-run this same experiment to confirm the improvement given deploy variance

description: Deploy with --no-bundle to skip wrangler worker bundling

asi.hypothesis: With most deps bundled via ssr.noExternal, wrangler --no-bundle could skip bundling entirely
asi.next_action_hint: Cannot use --no-bundle because SvelteKit adapter-cloudflare produces _worker.js with relative imports to manifest.js and server/index.js. Wrangler requires bundling to inline these. Focus on further reducing wrangler bundling work by bundling ALL remaining external deps (including @sveltejs/kit if possible), or by re-running the current best config to confirm the 19.4s result
asi.rollback_reason: _worker.js is not being bundled by Wrangler but it is importing from another file. This will throw an error if deployed. Wrangler enforces bundling for multi-file workers.

description: Deploy with --skip-caching to skip asset caching

asi.hypothesis: --skip-caching skips asset caching on Cloudflare side, reducing validation overhead during deploy
asi.next_action_hint: --skip-caching caused ALL 70 files to be re-uploaded instead of just 18 changed files. Upload time increased from ~1.6s to ~2.2s. This flag is counter-productive. Continue with current best ssr.noExternal config. Need multiple runs to establish confidence in the 19.4s result.

description: Make local SQLite code conditional on import.meta.env.DEV to allow tree-shaking in production

asi.hypothesis: Guarding local SQLite path with import.meta.env.DEV lets Rollup dead-code-eliminate better-sqlite3 and node:fs/node:path in production builds
asi.next_action_hint: Try adding 'sideEffects': false to better-sqlite3 package.json (via patch) to enable Rollup to drop the bare import. Or try using build.rollupOptions.treeshake.moduleSideEffects:false carefully. Or revert and try a different optimization target.
asi.rollback_reason: Total time 26.3s is worse than baseline ~20.8s due to deploy variance. However, db.js shrank from ~128KB to 1.5KB. The import 'better-sqlite3' and node:path remain as bare imports because Rollup treats them as side-effectful. Need to either add sideEffects:false to better-sqlite3 or find another way to strip the bare imports.

description: Remove better-sqlite3 from server bundle via dev-mode guards and sideEffects patch

asi.hypothesis: Removing better-sqlite3 from server bundle via import.meta.env.DEV guards + sideEffects:false patch reduces worker bundle size and wrangler bundling time
asi.next_action_hint: Need to also remove node:fs/node:path from portal.js and report-store.js to fully eliminate wrangler node: polyfill overhead. Or focus on a different optimization target since deploy variance dominates.
asi.rollback_reason: Total time 20.8s equals baseline 20.8s. Build time slightly improved (8.08s vs 8.5s) but deploy variance masked any net improvement. Better-sqlite3 was successfully removed from bundle, proving the tree-shaking technique works.

description: Bundle @sveltejs/kit and its internal modules into SSR output via ssr.noExternal

asi.hypothesis: Bundling @sveltejs/kit into SSR output removes ~25 external import statements, reducing wrangler's deploy-time resolution and bundling work
asi.next_action_hint: Combine with better-sqlite3 removal (dev-mode guards + sideEffects patch) to eliminate the remaining large native dependency. Or try removing node:fs/node:path imports from portal.js and report-store.js to further reduce wrangler polyfill overhead.

description: Combine @sveltejs/kit bundling with better-sqlite3 removal via dev-mode guards

asi.hypothesis: Combining @sveltejs/kit bundling with better-sqlite3 removal should further reduce worker bundle size and wrangler bundling time
asi.next_action_hint: Deploy variance is too high to reliably measure this optimization with single-run experiments. Consider changing primary metric to build-only time, or run 3-5 iterations per experiment and use median. The better-sqlite3 removal is proven to work (db.js shrinks to ~1.5KB) but deploy noise dominates total time.
asi.rollback_reason: Total time 26.3s is worse than baseline 20.0s due to deploy variance (upload 3.44s vs 1.56s). Build time unchanged. Need multiple runs or build-only metric to properly evaluate.

name: Optimizing SvelteKit build time (excluding deploy variance)

description: Baseline build-only time at HEAD with @sveltejs/kit bundling

asi.hypothesis: Current build-only baseline is ~8.2s. Deploy variance made total-time optimization unreliable. Switching to build-only metric enables measuring real code optimizations.
asi.next_action_hint: Try build optimizations that target server build time (~7s): reduce bundle size, remove dead code, or speed up Rollup. Server build dominates total build time.

description: Remove better-sqlite3 from server bundle to improve build time

asi.hypothesis: Removing better-sqlite3 from server bundle via dev-mode guards reduces Rollup bundling work and speeds up server build
asi.next_action_hint: better-sqlite3 removal does not improve build time. The module was small and Rollup's conditional analysis may add overhead. Focus on other build-time optimizations: reduce number of routes/components, optimize heavy dependencies, or try rollupOptions.manualChunks.
asi.rollback_reason: Build time 8.6s vs baseline 8.2s — no improvement. Removing better-sqlite3 doesn't speed up Vite build because the module was small. The win is in deploy time, not build time.

description: Disable minification to speed up Vite build

asi.hypothesis: Disabling minification (build.minify: false) speeds up Vite build by skipping esbuild minification step
asi.next_action_hint: Minification is not a bottleneck. In fact, larger output files without minification slow down disk writes and adapter processing. Keep minification enabled. Try other optimizations like reducing module count or disabling sourcemaps/CSS splitting.
asi.rollback_reason: Build time 8.4s-9.6s vs baseline 8.2s — worse. Disabling minification increases output size, slowing disk writes and adapter processing more than esbuild minification costs.
